pages.raw_text:
A conflict on the floor of the city
council last night between Council
man Schiller and Contractor Lii
lienthal was the bitterest clash
seen there in many months.


Schiller, who got up the remon-
strances against paving First av. in
Brownies addition with oileroid,
Lillienthals patent paving material,
got the floor and made strong
talk, attacking the material as I'M
sanitary and uncertain. He said
that the property owners did not
want an experimental paving in the
most beautiful part of the city. He
asked that the assessment rolls
prepared be not confirmed.


Lillienthal then got the floor.
During Mr.. Schiller's talk he stated
that Lillienthal had agreed to
smear" asphalt over the oileroid
if the property owners desired This
caused Lillienthal to get personal
He said that Schiller talked of
smearing asphalt as though talking
Of smearing labels on whiskey bot
ties. Schiller is in the liquor bust
ness. He further stated that he
would agree to cover his pavement
with asphalt if anyone who knew
anything about paving was not sat
isfied with the durability, ETC., and
not a man who knew only about
selling wines, whiskeys and cigars


Schiller demanded that Lillien-
thai either leave the floor or refrain
from personalities. And if the
president don't make him stop it,"
he said, "Ill go over there and
slap his face


Lillienthal replied: The whis-
key dealer isn't man enough TO slap
my face. | dare him to come over
here and see what I'll do to him."


President Phillips ordered LII
lienthal off the floor


Schiller demanded the floor and
launched into a direct attack. He
said Lillienthal had lied to prop
erty owners when he first got up
his petition.


II demand" began Lillienthal
again, and President Phillips COM
manded, Shut up" Lillienthal
shut up.


Just where Phillips gets his par
liamentary rules is mystery.
Schiller's motion that oileroid as
sessment rolls be not confirmed
was then put on Lamberts second.
Schiller and Lambert voted for it
and Funk and Dalke voted against
it.


Motion carries" said Phillips.
There were couple of grunts
voted on each side, but they were
equally divided.


After the meeting Lillienthal
stated that despite Schiller's oppo-
sition he would lay oileroid paving
in this town.
